response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned generalist software developer with 15+ years of experience, strong GIS/geospatial background via GISCollective, and exposure to low-latency systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ work at National Instruments a decade ago (2014-2015) with no Rust experience. Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. GIS domain fit and distributed-systems exposure are positives, but the core language requirement is a significant gap. Also currently on a caregiving career gap as of October 2025.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective platform and Green Map work
Exposure to low-latency, high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of req/sec)
Solid engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, Linux server applications
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (only ~6 months at NI in 2014-2015) and no Rust experience
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not the C++/Rust required for routing tile pipelines
No demonstrated experience with navigation/routing systems or production map data pipelines
